meaning: n.不一致,变化,变异,变迁,分歧,不和
examples: Many of his statements were at variance with the facts.
These conclusions are totally at variance with the evidence.
Although qualitative variance among nerve energies was never rigidly disproved, the doctrine was generally abandoned in favor of the opposing view.
First, there is surprisingly little variance in strategy among universities, in the U.S. in particular.
What separated life from death in their adventure was not the average, but the variance.
